methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The ePlane Company is a Series B transportation startup founded by Satya Chakravarthy, currently seeking $40–50M in fresh capital on the back of $14M+ in prior fundraising. The broader transportation sector comp universe consists of seven private firms—including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Arc Boats, and DeepDive—with no public comparables identified.

Valuation analysis relied on a single applicable method: stage-progression. Using the $14M total raised as a proxy for current post-money valuation, a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $27M. This multiplier reflects market-observed step-ups across similar funding rounds in the sector. Peer-median methodology was not applicable; while three transportation peers exist in the comp universe, none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier alignment—insufficient density to yield a reliable median.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M (low) to $31M (high), with $27M as the central estimate. This single-method triangulation carries material constraint: lack of same-stage or tightly comparable private peers limits cross-validation. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of the 1.9x multiplier in the current funding environment and whether the $14M proxy accurately reflects the company's negotiated post-money valuation at the last close. A more robust range would require either tighter peer discovery or additional methodological inputs.
